page.title: Email Server
page.subtitle: Set up a self-hosted mail server with a few clicks

removeModal.title: Remove this mail server?
removeModal.description: Removes {label} from the mail list only. The mail stack keeps running and nothing is uninstalled — re-add it anytime with "Adopt" for an existing install.
removeModal.body: Because the on-server install is left intact, re-adopting it later restores full management with no reinstall.

adopt.title: Adopt existing mail server
adopt.intro: Already set up a mail server with Openship on one of your servers? Pick it and scan — if a mail stack is found, it's re-adopted into the dashboard without reinstalling or touching your data.
adopt.noInstall: No mail server installation was found on this server.
adopt.scanFailed: Scan failed — is the server reachable?

dns.heading: Publish DNS records before continuing
dns.bodyBefore: Mail delivery breaks without these. Add them at your DNS provider, then click
dns.bodyAfterResume: below. The install resumes from step {resumeStep} (SSL certificate).
dns.autoConfigureDesc: Pick the provider hosting your domain's DNS. We'll push the records for you using their API.
dns.otherManualDesc: Copy each record from the banner and paste into your provider's UI
dns.comingSoonBefore: Direct API integration with this provider isn't shipped yet. For now, copy each record from the banner and paste it into your DNS provider's UI. Once that's done, click

providers.cloudflare: Free DNS, fast propagation. Most common choice.
providers.hostinger: If you bought the domain through Hostinger.

progress.resetTitle: Wipe install state on the server and start fresh
progress.noBuffered: No buffered output for this run.
progress.willStream: Logs will stream here once setup starts.

setup.title: Mail Server Setup
setup.subtitle: Deploy a complete self-hosted mail stack on this server
setup.willBeAtBefore: Your mail server will be at
setup.passwordHint: Used to log into the mailbox after setup. You can change it later from the admin panel below.

relay.hint: Recommended. Send via SES's trusted IPs (fresh-server IPs are often blocklisted, and many hosts block outbound port 25). Receiving stays on this server.
relay.perDomainNote: Applies to all domains. Per-domain routing + each domain's SES DKIM records are configured after install in the Sending tab.

setup.adoptCta: Already have a mail server? Adopt it
setup.prereq2: Port 25 not blocked by your provider

portConflict.title: Port Conflict Detected
portConflict.subtitle: Ports 80/443 are needed for the mail server
portConflict.killWarning: Make sure this process is not needed before killing it. This action cannot be undone.
portConflict.rerun: Re-run the port check and continue the setup.

dnsRef.title: Required DNS Records
dnsRef.subtitle: Reference - tap to view the records you published

steps.title: All steps
steps.interrupted: Install was interrupted before step {step}. Resume to pick up where it left off.

ptr.heading: Set reverse DNS (PTR) before continuing
ptr.bodyAfter: at your DNS provider. Without matching rDNS, Gmail and Outlook reject your outbound mail. The install resumes from step {resumeStep} (SSL).
ptr.howToBody: log into your VPS provider, find the server's networking / DNS / rDNS settings, and set the PTR record for each IP to
